justifications.clarity: The opening loan-officer vignette (lines 29-38) immediately grounds the abstract explainability problem in a concrete regulatory scenario, and every subsequent section builds on that frame with well-signposted transitions; the only friction is a dense cluster of regulatory acronyms (ECOA, FCRA, GDPR, MiFID II) introduced in rapid succession at lines 97-100 without a consolidated glossary.
justifications.rigor: The Shapley value formula (Eq. 12.1) is axiomatically grounded and the LIME and faithfulness metrics are formally specified, but the counterfactual generation algorithm for discrete text inputs (lines 479-486) is described only in narrative prose — 'identify the feature dimensions... then articulate those perturbations' — without any algorithmic pseudocode or complexity analysis, leaving the core technical procedure underspecified.
justifications.completeness: All six stated learning objectives are addressed, and the three case studies cover credit scoring, ECOA denial letters, and MiFID II suitability; however, Integrated Gradients is invoked as the preferred audit-grade method (lines 392-396 and line 688) without ever being formally defined or exemplified, and global XAI measures relevant to SR 11-7 model documentation (PDP, global SHAP summaries) are entirely absent.
justifications.pedagogy: Learning objectives are enumerated at the outset, difficulty escalates from regulatory context through formal methods to applied case studies, and the three worked examples scaffold each other effectively; the sole structural weakness is the complete absence of end-of-chapter exercises, which the book convention requires in [B]/[I]/[A] difficulty tiers.
justifications.style: Notation ($\phi_i$, $f(x)$, $\mathcal{G}$, $\pi_x$) is consistent throughout and the prose avoids AI-sounding padding, but the bibliography key 'sundararajan2020shapley' misidentifies the Integrated Gradients paper (whose title is 'Axiomatic Attribution for Deep Networks'), and the \textbf{Stage N} inline headers inside the hybrid-pipeline example (lines 556-558) inconsistently replicate a paragraph-labelling style that the rest of the chapter deliberately avoids.
